title: Boat Rental in Oslo Fjord: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Exploring the Oslo Fjord by water does not have to break the bank. From the bustling Aker Brygge marina, you can rent a motorboat for a half-day for approximately EUR 250, or join a shared RIB tour from EUR 80. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local owners to find the best value.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Oslo Fjord?

Prices vary widely but budget travellers can start from EUR 80 for a shared tour and EUR 250 for a self-drive motorboat half-day. Chartering a crewed sailing yacht for a full day typically runs EUR 800 to EUR 1,500, while a luxury motor yacht like an Azimut 50 can exceed EUR 2,500 per day. For the most accurate and current rates, you can browse the full range of options through a yacht charter in Oslo Fjord on BoatHire24, where filters let you sort by price and boat type.

Where to set off in Oslo Fjord

The best departure points are well-served marinas within easy reach of the city centre. Aker Brygge is the most central, with frequent ferries and boat rentals. Other excellent harbours include Vippetangen, Bygdøy, and Frognerkilen. For those wanting to explore the outer fjord, head to Drøbak or Nesodden, where smaller marinas offer direct access to the open water and islands such as Håøya and the Hvaler archipelago.

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right boat depends on your group size and the experience you want. Here are five popular options with indicative prices:

  1. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), best for speed and island-hopping, up to 6 guests, from EUR 80 per person for a shared tour.
  2. Small motorboat, ideal for a private half-day exploring quiet coves, up to 5 guests, from EUR 250 for 4 hours.
  3. Sailing yacht, perfect for a relaxed full-day sail, up to 8 guests, from EUR 500 half-day or EUR 800 full day.
  4. Catamaran, spacious and stable for families, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,200 for a full day.
  5. Cabin cruiser / motor yacht, for a premium experience with overnight capability, models like the Prestige 500, see the Prestige 500 charter page for full specifications and availability.

Cost breakdown for a budget trip

Compare these typical price bands to plan your budget in EUR.

  • Shared RIB tour: EUR 80 per adult, no licence required.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): EUR 250, licence required unless you take a skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): EUR 1,200, skipper included.
  • Crewed sailing yacht full-day (up to 8 guests): EUR 800, includes fuel and lunch.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Oslo Fjord?

For motorboats over 8 metres or 25 knots, you generally need a Norwegian boat licence or an equivalent ICC. RIB and small boat rentals often include a brief briefing or optional skipper for an extra EUR 50. The exact requirements vary by operator, so always confirm when booking. In 2026, the high season runs from June to August, when the prevailing southerly wind, known as the 'sørøst', can kick up a short chop in the outer fjord.

A local tip: start early in the morning to secure a prime mooring at the popular island of Hovedøya, where the beach and café open from 10:00. The inner fjord is usually calmer before noon.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 80, 1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: half-day to full day (4, 8 hours)
  • Maximum guests: 12 on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR (Euro)

Questions about boat rental in Oslo Fjord

Can I rent a boat in Oslo without a licence?

Yes, many operators offer small motorboats under 8 metres that require only a brief safety briefing, or you can hire a local skipper for an additional fee.

What is the best month for a boat trip in Oslo Fjord?

July offers the warmest weather and longest daylight hours, but June and August are also excellent with fewer crowds.

How long does it take to sail from Aker Brygge to the Hvaler islands?

It takes roughly two to three hours by motorboat to cover the 35 nautical miles southeast to the Hvaler archipelago, so plan a full-day trip.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a boat?

Fuel is often extra, so ask for an estimate based on your planned route; mooring fees at islands like Hankø are usually included in the charter.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Oslo Fjord or list your own. Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, you can find verified boats from local owners on the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat in the fjord, you can also list it to earn extra income during the busy 2026 season.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Oslo Fjord and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
